message.content:
Explore o código para entender:

1. Como o App.tsx busca tasks (fetchProjectFeatures)
2. O que o harness CLI espera (qual banco/formato)
3. Por que tasks podem não aparecer
4. Se existe opção de adicionar tasks no UI

Foque em:
- src/App.tsx (fetchProjectFeatures)
- src-tauri/src/lib.rs (run_harness_command)
- src-tauri/src/bin/harness.rs (CLI commands)

Retorne um resumo do fluxo e o que está faltando.

content.text: Vou explorar o código para entender o fluxo de tasks e identificar o que está faltando.

  {
    "id": "F012",
    "description": "Native UX Polish - Make the app feel like a native macOS application, not a web app",
    "category": "ux",
    "priority": "high",
    "status": "done",
    "passes": true,
    "estimatedEffort": "9 hours",
    "platform": "macOS only",
    "steps": [
      "Phase 0: Native Menu Bar - Create macOS-style menu (HQ, File, Edit, View, Window, Help) in src-tauri/src/lib.rs using Tauri Menu API",

      "phase0_native_menu": {
        "description": "Native Menu Bar (macOS style)",
        "effort": "1 hour",
        "files": ["src-tauri/src/lib.rs"],
        "details": {
          "problem": "Current menu looks like web app (HQ, Edit...), not native macOS",
          "solution": "Use Tauri Menu API with PredefinedMenuItem for standard macOS menus",
          "menus": ["HQ (About, Settings, Services, Hide, Quit)", "File (Open Project, New Tab, Close Tab)", "Edit (Undo, Redo, Cut, Copy, Paste, Select All)", "View (Toggle Sidebar, Toggle Logs, Fullscreen)", "Window (Minimize, Maximize, Prev/Next Tab)", "Help (Documentation, GitHub)"]
        }

        "description": "CSS Foundation (No dependencies)",
        "effort": "30 min",
        "files": ["src/index.css"],
        "details": {
          "font": "ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system at 14px (native apps use 13-14px, not 16px)",
          "userSelect": "none on all UI elements, text only on content (.selectable, pre, code, .log-content)",

          "focus": ":focus { outline: none } + :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid ... }",

          "overscroll": "overscroll-behavior: none to prevent bounce",

        "description": "Disable Browser Behaviors",

          "devMode": "Keep DEV_TOOLS and RELOAD enabled in debug mode",
          "usage": "tauri_plugin_prevent_default::Builder::new().with_flags(Flags::all() - Flags::DEV_TOOLS - Flags::RELOAD).build()"
        }
      },

        "description": "Window Vibrancy (macOS blur effect)",
        "effort": "45 min",
        "files": ["src-tauri/Cargo.toml", "src-tauri/tauri.conf.json", "src-tauri/src/lib.rs"],

            "windows": [{ "transparent": true, "decorations": true, "titleBarStyle": "Overlay", "hiddenTitle": true }]
          },
          "rustCode": "apply_vibrancy(&window, NSVisualEffectMaterial::Sidebar, None, None)",
          "materials": ["Sidebar (recommended)", "HudWindow (darker)", "FullScreenUI", "ContentBackground"],
          "limitation": "macOS only, Windows/Linux will have solid background"
        }

          "inspiration": "Notion, Linear - custom rendered menus, not OS native"
        }
      },
      "phase5_keybindings": {
        "description": "Configurable Keybindings (VSCode-style)",
        "effort": "2 hours",
        "files": ["src/config/defaultKeybindings.json", "src/config/keybindingsSchema.ts", "src/hooks/useKeybindings.ts"],
        "details": {
          "format": "VSCode keybindings.json: { key, command, when?, args? }",
          "defaultShortcuts": {
            "cmd+t": "tab.new",
            "cmd+w": "tab.close",
            "cmd+shift+[": "tab.previous",
            "cmd+shift+]": "tab.next",
            "cmd+,": "settings.open",
            "cmd+k": "commandPalette.open",
            "cmd+\\": "sidebar.toggle",
            "cmd+j": "logs.toggle",
            "cmd+o": "project.open",
            "escape": "modal.close (when: modalOpen)"
          },
          "userConfig": "~/.config/hq/keybindings.json for user overrides",
          "features": ["Command registry pattern", "When clause for context-aware shortcuts", "Hot-reload on file change", "Merge user overrides with defaults"]
        }
      },
      "phase6_animations": {
        "description": "Spring Animations",
        "effort": "2 hours",
        "files": ["package.json", "src/components/*.tsx"],
        "details": {
          "library": "motion (formerly Framer Motion)",
          "install": "bun add motion",
          "animations": {
            "tabSwitch": "spring with stiffness: 400, damping: 30",
            "cardExpand": "type: spring, bounce: 0.2",
            "panelResize": "type: tween, ease: easeOut",
            "fadeIn": "initial: { opacity: 0, y: 10 }, animate: { opacity: 1, y: 0 }"
          },
          "why": "Physical spring physics feel native, not 'webby' easing curves"
        }
      },
      "phase7_focus_blur": {
        "description": "Focus/Blur Window State",
        "effort": "45 min",
        "files": ["src-tauri/src/lib.rs", "src/hooks/useWindowFocus.ts", "src/index.css"],
        "details": {
          "rustEvent": "window.on_window_event for Focused(bool), emit 'window-focus' to frontend",
          "frontendHook": "useWindowFocus() returns isFocused, adds .window-unfocused class to html",
          "cssEffect": "Dim colors when unfocused: .window-unfocused .tab-active { background: gray }",
          "inspiration": "Native macOS apps dim selection highlight when window loses focus"
        }
      }
    },
    "dependencies": {
      "rust": ["window-vibrancy = \"0.7\"", "tauri-plugin-prevent-default = \"2.1\""],
      "npm": ["motion"]
    },
    "filesToCreate": [
      "src/config/defaultKeybindings.json",
      "src/config/keybindingsSchema.ts",
      "src/hooks/useKeybindings.ts",
      "src/hooks/useWindowFocus.ts",
      "src/components/ContextMenu.tsx"
    ],
    "filesToModify": [
      "src-tauri/Cargo.toml",
      "src-tauri/tauri.conf.json",
      "src-tauri/src/lib.rs",
      "src/index.css",
      "package.json"

      "https://github.com/ferreira-tb/tauri-plugin-prevent-default",
      "https://getlotus.app/21-making-electron-apps-feel-native-on-mac",
      "https://motion.dev/"
    ],
    "notes": {
      "platform": "macOS only for this implementation",
      "inspiration": ["Linear", "Notion", "Warp", "Arc", "Raycast", "VSCode"],
      "knownLimitations": [
        "Vibrancy is macOS-only",

        "DevTools kept in dev mode for debugging"
      ]
    }
  },

   240→================================================================================
   241→TASK: F011 - Dev Bridge (Browser-to-Native RPC)
   242→================================================================================
   243→
   244→## Problem Statement
   245→
   246→Tauri's `invoke()` function only works inside the native webview context
   247→(when the app runs via `bun tauri dev` and opens in the Tauri window).
   248→
   249→When testing via Playwright MCP, the browser loads `localhost:5173` directly,
   250→which is the Vite dev server WITHOUT Tauri's native layer. This means:
   251→
   252→### What DOES NOT work in browser mode:
   253→- `@tauri-apps/api/core` invoke() - TypeError: Cannot read 'invoke'
   254→- PTY process spawning (pty_spawn, pty_write, pty_kill)
   255→- File system operations (read/write project files)
   256→- Native dialogs (file picker, save dialog)
   257→- System notifications
   258→- Any Rust backend functionality
   259→
   260→### What DOES work in browser mode:
   261→- React components and UI rendering
   262→- State management (@zazcart/tools)
   263→- Mock data and simulated interactions
   264→- PatternFly components
   265→- Pure frontend logic
   266→
   267→## Why This Matters
   268→
   269→1. **Testing Gap**: Cannot verify PTY, file watching, or native features via Playwright
   270→2. **Development Friction**: Must manually test in Tauri window for native features
   271→3. **CI/CD Limitation**: Automated tests cannot cover critical functionality
   272→
   273→## Proposed Solution: Dev Bridge
   274→
   275→Create a development-only RPC bridge that:
   276→
   277→```
   278→┌─────────────────┐     WebSocket/HTTP      ┌─────────────────┐
   279→│  Browser        │ ◄─────────────────────► │  Rust Dev       │
   280→│  (Playwright)   │     localhost:9876      │  Server         │
   281→│                 │     + auth token        │                 │
   282→│  TypeScript     │                         │  Mirrors Tauri  │
   283→│  RPC Client     │                         │  Commands       │
   284→└─────────────────┘                         └─────────────────┘
   285→```
   286→
   287→### Architecture
   288→
   289→1. **Rust Dev Server** (only compiled in debug mode):
   290→   - Binds to localhost:9876 (or configurable port)
   291→   - Accepts WebSocket connections with token auth
   292→   - Exposes same commands as Tauri invoke (pty_spawn, etc.)
   293→   - Streams events back to client (pty-output, pty-exit)
   294→
   295→2. **TypeScript Client** (unified API):
   296→   ```typescript
   297→   // Auto-detects context and routes appropriately
   298→   async function invokeCommand(cmd: string, args: object) {
   299→     if (isTauri()) {
   300→       return await invoke(cmd, args);  // Native path
   301→     } else if (isDevBridge()) {
   302→       return await rpcClient.call(cmd, args);  // Bridge path
   303→     } else {
   304→       throw new Error('No backend available');
   305→     }
   306→   }
   307→   ```
   308→
   309→3. **Security Measures**:
   310→   - localhost-only binding (127.0.0.1, not 0.0.0.0)
   311→   - Token generated at startup, passed via env/file
   312→   - Disabled entirely in release builds (#[cfg(debug_assertions)])
   313→   - Rate limiting to prevent abuse
   314→   - Command allowlist (no arbitrary execution)
   315→
   316→### Future SDK Consideration
   317→
   318→The RPC pattern established here can later be extended for plugins:
   319→- Plugins run in sandboxed context (iframe, worker, or separate process)
   320→- Communicate with host via same RPC protocol
   321→- Host controls which commands plugins can invoke
   322→- No need to redesign later; just add permission layer
   323→
   324→**IMPORTANT**: Do NOT over-engineer for plugins now. Just ensure the bridge
   325→design doesn't preclude adding a permission/sandbox layer later.
   326→
   327→## Implementation Steps
   328→
   329→See F011 steps in features.json. Estimated complexity: Medium-High.

TASK: F011 - Dev Bridge (Browser-to-Native RPC)
================================================================================

## Problem Statement

Tauri's `invoke()` function only works inside the native webview context
(when the app runs via `bun tauri dev` and opens in the Tauri window).

When testing via Playwright MCP, the browser loads `localhost:5173` directly,
which is the Vite dev server WITHOUT Tauri's native layer. This means:

### What DOES NOT work in browser mode:
- `@tauri-apps/api/core` invoke() - TypeError: Cannot read 'invoke'
- PTY process spawning (pty_spawn, pty_write, pty_kill)
- File system operations (read/write project files)
- Native dialogs (file picker, save dialog)
- System notifications
- Any Rust backend functionality

### What DOES work in browser mode:
- React components and UI rendering
- State management (@zazcart/tools)
- Mock data and simulated interactions
- PatternFly components
- Pure frontend logic

## Why This Matters

1. **Testing Gap**: Cannot verify PTY, file watching, or native features via Playwright
2. **Development Friction**: Must manually test in Tauri window for native features
3. **CI/CD Limitation**: Automated tests cannot cover critical functionality

## Proposed Solution: Dev Bridge

Create a development-only RPC bridge that:

```
┌─────────────────┐     WebSocket/HTTP      ┌─────────────────┐
│  Browser        │ ◄─────────────────────► │  Rust Dev       │
│  (Playwright)   │     localhost:9876      │  Server         │
│                 │     + auth token        │                 │
│  TypeScript     │                         │  Mirrors Tauri  │
│  RPC Client     │                         │  Commands       │
└─────────────────┘                         └─────────────────┘
```

### Architecture

1. **Rust Dev Server** (only compiled in debug mode):
   - Binds to localhost:9876 (or configurable port)
   - Accepts WebSocket connections with token auth
   - Exposes same commands as Tauri invoke (pty_spawn, etc.)
   - Streams events back to client (pty-output, pty-exit)

2. **TypeScript Client** (unified API):
   ```typescript
   // Auto-detects context and routes appropriately
   async function invokeCommand(cmd: string, args: object) {
     if (isTauri()) {
       return await invoke(cmd, args);  // Native path
     } else if (isDevBridge()) {
       return await rpcClient.call(cmd, args);  // Bridge path
     } else {
       throw new Error('No backend available');
     }
   }
   ```

3. **Security Measures**:
   - localhost-only binding (127.0.0.1, not 0.0.0.0)
   - Token generated at startup, passed via env/file
   - Disabled entirely in release builds (#[cfg(debug_assertions)])
   - Rate limiting to prevent abuse
   - Command allowlist (no arbitrary execution)

### Future SDK Consideration

The RPC pattern established here can later be extended for plugins:
- Plugins run in sandboxed context (iframe, worker, or separate process)
- Communicate with host via same RPC protocol
- Host controls which commands plugins can invoke
- No need to redesign later; just add permission layer

**IMPORTANT**: Do NOT over-engineer for plugins now. Just ensure the bridge
design doesn't preclude adding a permission/sandbox layer later.

## Implementation Steps

See F011 steps in features.json. Estimated complexity: Medium-High.

     6→//! ## Usage

   481→
   482→impl std::fmt::Display for CliError {

   484→        match self {
   485→            CliError::NotHarnessProject => write!(f, "Not a Harness project (no .harness/ folder)"),

   512→fn cmd_init(project_dir: &PathBuf, format: OutputFormat) -> CliResult<()> {
   513→    let harness_dir = project_dir.join(db::HARNESS_FOLDER);
   514→
   515→    // Create .harness/ if it doesn't exist
   516→    if !harness_dir.exists() {
   517→        std::fs::create_dir_all(&harness_dir)?;
   518→    }
   519→
   520→    // Open or create the database
   521→    let conn = db::open_or_create(project_dir)?;
   522→
   523→    let db_path = db::get_db_path(project_dir);
   524→
   525→    match format {
   526→        OutputFormat::Json => {
   527→            println!(
   528→                r#"{{"success":true,"path":"{}","version":"{}"}}"#,
   529→                db_path.display(),
   530→                db::SCHEMA_VERSION
   531→            );
   532→        }
   533→        OutputFormat::Quiet => {
   534→            println!("{}", db_path.display());
   535→        }
   536→        OutputFormat::Text => {
   537→            println!("Initialized Harness database at {}", db_path.display());
   538→            println!("Schema version: {}", db::SCHEMA_VERSION);
   539→
   540→            // Show stats
   541→            let stats = db::DbStats::collect(&conn, Some(&db_path))?;
   542→            println!("\nDatabase stats:");
   543→            println!("  Tasks:    {}", stats.task_count);
   544→            println!("  Steps:    {}", stats.step_count);
   545→            println!("  Sessions: {}", stats.session_count);

   548→    }

   551→}

   553→fn cmd_db_status(project_dir: &PathBuf, format: OutputFormat) -> CliResult<()> {

   556→    }
   557→
   558→    let conn = db::open(project_dir)?;
   559→    let db_path = db::get_db_path(project_dir);
   560→    let stats = db::DbStats::collect(&conn, Some(&db_path))?;
   561→    let version = db::get_schema_version(&conn)?.unwrap_or_else(|| "unknown".to_string());

   595→fn cmd_task(project_dir: &PathBuf, command: TaskCommands, format: OutputFormat) -> CliResult<()> {

   601→
   602→    match command {

   604→            title,
   605→            description,
   606→            category,
   607→            status,
   608→        } => {
   609→            let mut task = db::ProjectTask::new(&title).with_status(status.into());
   610→
   611→            if let Some(ref desc) = description {
   612→                task = task.with_description(desc);
   613→            }
   614→            if let Some(ref cat) = category {
   615→                task = task.with_category(cat);
   616→            }
   617→
   618→            db::create_task(&conn, &task)?;
   619→
   620→            match format {
   621→                OutputFormat::Json => {
   622→                    println!("{}", serde_json::to_string(&task).unwrap_or_default());
   623→                }
   624→                OutputFormat::Quiet => {
   625→                    println!("{}", task.id);
   626→                }
   627→                OutputFormat::Text => {
   628→                    println!("Created task: {}", task.id);
   629→                    println!("  Title:  {}", task.title);
   630→                    println!("  Status: {}", task.status.as_str());
   631→                }
   632→            }
   633→        }
   634→
   635→        TaskCommands::List { status, limit, cursor } => {
   636→            let result = if let Some(status) = status {
   637→                db::list_tasks_by_status(&conn, status.into(), cursor.as_deref(), Some(limit))?
   638→            } else {
   639→                db::list_tasks(&conn, cursor.as_deref(), Some(limit))?
   640→            };
   641→
   642→            match format {
   643→                OutputFormat::Json => {
   644→                    println!("{}", serde_json::to_string(&result).unwrap_or_default());
   645→                }
   646→                OutputFormat::Quiet => {
   647→                    for task in &result.items {
   648→                        println!("{}", task.id);
   649→                    }
   650→                }
   651→                OutputFormat::Text => {
   652→                    if result.items.is_empty() {
   653→                        println!("No tasks found.");
   654→                    } else {
   655→                        println!("{:<26}  {:<8}  {}", "ID", "STATUS", "TITLE");
   656→                        println!("{}", "-".repeat(70));

   658→                            println!(
   659→                                "{:<26}  {:<8}  {}",
   660→                                task.id,
   661→                                task.status.as_str(),
   662→                                truncate(&task.title, 32)
   663→                            );

   668→                        }
   669→                    }
   670→                }
   671→            }
   672→        }
   673→
   674→        TaskCommands::Search { query, limit } => {
   675→            // Use semantic search for relevance-ordered results
   676→            let results = db::search_tasks_semantic(&conn, &query, Some(limit))?;
   677→
   678→            match format {
   679→                OutputFormat::Json => {
   680→                    // Extract just tasks for JSON output
   681→                    let tasks: Vec<_> = results.iter().map(|(t, _)| t).collect();
   682→                    println!("{}", serde_json::to_string(&tasks).unwrap_or_default());
   683→                }
   684→                OutputFormat::Quiet => {
   685→                    for (task, _) in &results {
   686→                        println!("{}", task.id);
   687→                    }
   688→                }
   689→                OutputFormat::Text => {
   690→                    if results.is_empty() {
   691→                        println!("No tasks found matching '{}'.", query);
   692→                    } else {
   693→                        println!("Found {} task(s) matching '{}' (ordered by relevance):", results.len(), query);
   694→                        println!();
   695→                        println!("{:<26}  {:<8}  {:<6}  {}", "ID", "STATUS", "SCORE", "TITLE");
   696→                        println!("{}", "-".repeat(80));

   699→                            let score = 1.0 - distance.min(1.0);
   700→                            println!(
   701→                                "{:<26}  {:<8}  {:.3}   {}",
   702→                                task.id,
   703→                                task.status.as_str(),
   704→                                score,
   705→                                truncate(&task.title, 32)
   706→                            );
   707→                        }
   708→                    }
   709→                }
   710→            }
   711→        }

   744→                    }
   745→                }
   746→            }
   747→        }
   748→
   749→        TaskCommands::Update {

   764→

content.text: Agora vou procurar especificamente como o `fetchProjectFeatures` obtém dados do harness CLI:

Perfect! Agora tenho a visão completa. Deixa eu preparar um resumo detalhado do fluxo e o que está faltando.

## RESUMO: Fluxo de Tasks e Problemas Identificados

### 1️⃣ COMO FUNCIONA ATUALMENTE

#### **Frontend (App.tsx, linhas 44-74)**
```typescript
async function fetchProjectFeatures(projectPath: string): Promise<Feature[]> {
  const result = await invoke<string>('run_harness_command', {
    projectPath,
    args: ['task', 'list', '--format', 'json'],
  });
  
  const tasks = JSON.parse(result);
  return tasks.map(task => ({...}));
}
```

**Fluxo:**
1. App chama `fetchProjectFeatures(projectPath)` quando projeto muda (useEffect linha 113-123)
2. Invoca comando Tauri `run_harness_command` com args `['task', 'list', '--format', 'json']`
3. Espera JSON de volta
4. Converte para array `Feature[]`
5. Renderiza no KanbanBoard (6 colunas: BACKLOG, TODO, DOING, REVIEW, QA, DONE)

#### **Backend (lib.rs, linhas 46-70)**
```rust
#[tauri::command]
fn run_harness_command(project_path: String, args: Vec<String>) -> Result<String, String> {
    let harness_path = current_exe()?.parent()?.join("harness");
    
    let output = Command::new(&harness_path)
        .current_dir(&project_path)
        .args(&args)
        .output()?;
        
    if output.status.success() {
        Ok(String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).to_string())
    } else {
        Err(format!("Harness command failed: {}", stderr))
    }
}
```

**Executa:** `./harness task list --format json` no diretório do projeto

#### **CLI (bin/harness.rs, linhas 635-672)**
```rust
TaskCommands::List { status, limit, cursor } => {
    let result = if let Some(status) = status {
        db::list_tasks_by_status(&conn, status.into(), cursor.as_deref(), Some(limit))?
    } else {
        db::list_tasks(&conn, cursor.as_deref(), Some(limit))?
    };
    
    // JSON format output
    if format == OutputFormat::Json {
        println!("{}", serde_json::to_string(&result).unwrap_or_default());
    }
}
```

**Banco de dados:** Consulta `project_tasks` table via SQLite

---

### 2️⃣ PROBLEMAS E O QUE ESTÁ FALTANDO

| Problema | Causa | Impacto | Status |
|----------|-------|--------|--------|
| **Tasks não aparecem no UI** | Usuário não tem banco DB criado (não rodou `harness init`) | Kanban exibe "No Tasks" | ❌ Não tratado |
| **Sem forma de adicionar tasks via UI** | Apenas o CLI pode adicionar (`harness task add`) | UX ruim - usuário precisa usar terminal | ❌ Falta Feature |
| **JSON format pode falhar silenciosamente** | App retorna `[]` se parse falhar (linha 72) | UI mostro "No Tasks" em vez de erro real | ⚠️ Tratamento fraco |
| **Sem validação da resposta do harness** | CLI pode retornar status inválido | Features mapeadas incorretamente | ⚠️ Sem validação |
| **Sem indicação visual que DB não existe** | `run_harness_command` falha silenciosamente | Usuário não sabe por que não há tasks | ❌ Não tratado |
| **Update/Delete tasks precisa de CLI** | Apenas comandos `harness task update/delete` | UX muito primitiva | ❌ Falta Feature |

---

### 3️⃣ FLUXO COMPLETO (como deveria ser)

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Usuário abre projeto via ProjectSelector                │
└────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────┘
             │
             ├─ Valida: projeto tem .harness/ folder?
             │          (check_harness_exists)
             │
             ├─ ✅ Sim → Verifica se DB existe
             │    └─ Não existe? Init automático (harness init)
             │    └─ Existe? Carrega tasks
             │
             └─ ❌ Não → Mostra erro
             
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ fetchProjectFeatures() é chamada                        │
└────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────┘
             │
             ├─ Executa: harness task list --format json
             │           (via run_harness_command)
             │
             ├─ Recebe JSON com tasks
             │           ex: [{id, title, status, ...}]
             │
             ├─ Mapeia para Feature[] (converte status → passes)
             │
             └─ Renderiza KanbanBoard com 6 colunas
```

---

### 4️⃣ DADOS ESPERADOS DO HARNESS CLI

**Comando:** `harness task list --format json`

**Resposta esperada:**
```json
{
  "items": [
    {
      "id": "01ARZ3NDEKTSV4RRFFQ69G5FAV",
      "title": "Implement auth",
      "description": "Add user login",
      "status": "DOING",
      "category": "core",
      "priority": "high",
      "is_passed": false,
      "created_at": "2026-01-04T10:00:00Z",
      "updated_at": "2026-01-04T10:00:00Z"
    }
  ],
  "has_next": false,
  "next_cursor": null
}
```

**Mapeamento para Feature:**
- `task.id` → `feature.id`
- `task.title` → `feature.description`
- `task.description` → `feature.steps[0]`
- `task.status` → `feature.status` (BACKLOG|TODO|DOING|REVIEW|QA|DONE)
- `task.is_passed === true` → `feature.passes = true` (vai para coluna DONE)
- `task.category` → `feature.category`
- `task.priority` → `feature.priority`

---

### 5️⃣ O QUE ESTÁ IMPLEMENTADO vs. O QUE FALTA

#### ✅ IMPLEMENTADO (14/16 features)
- F001: Tabs para múltiplos projetos
- F002: Kanban board (6 colunas)
- F003: Log panel com ANSI colors
- F004: PTY process controller
- F005: Progress card (mostra stats via harness CLI)
- F006: Project selector dialog
- F008: File watcher para .harness/
- F009: State management (@zazcart/tools)
- F010: Tauri + React 19
- F011: Dev Bridge (browser ↔ Rust RPC)
- F012: Native UX Polish
- F013: SQLite storage (harness.db)
- F014: harness CLI utility
- F015: Visual test screenshots
- F016: Semantic search

#### ❌ FALTA: UI para CRUD de Tasks
- Adicionar nova task via UI (botão)
- Editar task (modal com form)
- Deletar task (com confirmação)
- Mover task entre colunas (drag-and-drop)
- Marcar task como completa

#### ⚠️ TRATAMENTO FRACO
- Sem feedback quando DB não existe
- Sem error state no KanbanBoard
- Sem loading state claro
- JSON parse silencioso retorna `[]`

---

### 6️⃣ POR QUE TASKS NÃO APARECEM (Diagnóstico)

**Cenário comum:**
1. Usuário abre projeto via ProjectSelector
2. App chama `fetchProjectFeatures(projectPath)`
3. `run_harness_command(['task', 'list', '--format', 'json'])`
4. Harness CLI procura `.harness/harness.db` → **NÃO EXISTE**
5. CLI retorna erro: `"Not a Harness project (no .harness/ folder)"`
6. `run_harness_command` falha com `Err("Harness command failed: Not a Harness project...")`
7. App.tsx catch block (linha 70) ignora erro e retorna `[]`
8. KanbanBoard mostra "No Tasks" com mensagem genérica

**Solução necessária:**
```typescript
// Adicionar ao App.tsx quando projeto é selecionado:
const hasDatabase = await invoke<boolean>('check_harness_db_exists', {projectPath});
if (!hasDatabase) {
  await invoke<string>('run_harness_command', {
    projectPath,
    args: ['init']
  });
}
```

---

### 7️⃣ RESUMO FINAL

| Aspecto | Status | Detalhes |
|---------|--------|----------|
| **Leitura de tasks** | ✅ Funciona | Via `harness task list --format json` |
| **Renderização no Kanban** | ✅ Funciona | 6 colunas, cards expandíveis |
| **Adição de tasks** | ❌ Falta | Apenas CLI: `harness task add "Title"` |
| **Edição de tasks** | ❌ Falta | Apenas CLI: `harness task update <id>` |
| **Exclusão de tasks** | ❌ Falta | Apenas CLI: `harness task delete <id>` |
| **Validação de DB** | ⚠️ Fraco | Sem `harness init` automático |
| **Tratamento de erros** | ⚠️ Fraco | Retorna `[]` silenciosamente |
